Variables.Name: LevelSequenceAsset
Variables.Description: A level sequence asset to playback at runtime - used where the level sequence does not already exist in the world.

Variables.Name: bUseCustomStartFrame
Variables.Description: When enabled, the StartFrame setting will override the default starting frame number

Variables.Name: StartFrame
Variables.Description: Frame number to start capturing.  The frame number range depends on whether the bUseRelativeFrameNumbers option is enabled.

Variables.Name: bUseCustomEndFrame
Variables.Description: When enabled, the EndFrame setting will override the default ending frame number

Variables.Name: EndFrame
Variables.Description: Frame number to end capturing.  The frame number range depends on whether the bUseRelativeFrameNumbers option is enabled.

Variables.Name: WarmUpFrameCount
Variables.Description:
The number of extra frames to play before the sequence's start frame, to "warm up" the animation.  This is useful if your
	    animation contains particles or other runtime effects that are spawned into the scene earlier than your capture start frame

Variables.Name: DelayBeforeWarmUp
Variables.Description: The number of seconds to wait (in real-time) before we start playing back the warm up frames.  Useful for allowing post processing effects to settle down before capturing the animation.

Variables.Name: bWriteEditDecisionList
Variables.Description: Whether to write edit decision lists (EDLs) if the sequence contains shots

Variables.Name: LevelSequenceActor
Variables.Description: The pre-existing level sequence actor to use for capture that specifies playback settings
